document.critique: Tested all five candidates against machine counts and every interaction. Candidates 1–4 each fail at least one exact-one or implication constraint. Candidate 0 satisfies all constraints, has three jobs per machine, and its costs sum exactly to 140. It therefore replaces the preceding non-proposal as the strongest surviving answer.

Audited every candidate against capacities and interactions. Candidates 0 and 1 are feasible but cost 184 and 168. Candidate 2 violates capacity and J2/J11 separation; candidate 4 violates “J3=Z implies J9≠X.” Candidate 3 satisfies all constraints with three jobs per machine and costs 166, so it is the strongest surviving minimum among the supplied proposals.

Checked all capacity, domain, interaction, implication, and exact-one constraints. This assignment is feasible and its costs sum to 114; candidate 0’s 93 is arithmetically false, while candidates 2 and 3 cost 162 and 118. Candidate 4 is also feasible at 114, so the stated uniqueness is contradicted; this answer retains one minimum assignment.

Tested all listed candidates against machine counts, interaction rules, and exact costs. Candidate 2 survives every constraint: each machine has three jobs, and its cost sums to 174. The other candidates either violate capacity or constraints, or have higher cost. Thus this feasible 174 assignment is retained as the strongest answer.

I re-evaluated every candidate against the full nested Boolean statements; each listed candidate fails at least one decisive person (notably Candidate 4 fails at E). Solving the resulting truth-value constraints gives the assignment returned here. Direct substitution makes each truthful statement true and each liar statement false, so this exact answer replaces the preceding proposal.

I tested each candidate against the speaker/type equivalence. Candidate 0 fails at D; candidate 2 fails at F; candidate 3 fails at E. Candidate 1 is otherwise consistent, but its O=liar label contradicts O’s statement, which evaluates true. Correcting only O to truthful yields a fully consistent assignment, so it replaces the candidates.

I re-evaluated all candidates under the exact Boolean rules. Candidates 0–2 make C’s implication true while labeling C a liar; candidates 3–4 make E’s XOR false while labeling E truthful. Rechecking the resulting fixed point gives E,F,G,K,L,M,O,Q liars and all others truthful; every statement then matches its speaker’s type, so this replaces the preceding proposal.

Recomputed every nested statement under the surviving candidates. Candidate 4 satisfies all listed type/statement equivalences except K: with L=false, G=true, and F=true, K’s statement is AND(true, XOR(false,true))=true, contradicting K as a liar. Thus no candidate survives exactly; candidate 4 is retained only as the closest proposal, with this decisive inconsistency noted.

Rechecked the recurrence across the supplied terms and falsified Candidates 1 and 2 immediately: Candidate 1 fails the established n=20 relation, while Candidate 2 is inconsistent with the nonzero observed structure. Candidate 3 satisfies that final relation too, but does not survive the full periodic-coefficient verification. Candidate 0 is the unique surviving continuation.

I rechecked the recurrence’s periodic indexing and the n=4, 8, 12, and 16 constraints, including modular differences and the n−3 terms. Several tempting coefficient combinations fail those congruences; no preceding proposal was available to retain. Candidate 3 is the surviving exact continuation.

I rechecked the recurrence using the repeated n mod 4 equations, including modular differences across n=4,8,12,16 and all three possible moduli. The constraints do not cleanly reproduce a valid coefficient tuple as written, suggesting an indexing or transcription inconsistency. Of the shuffled candidates, [44,51,10] is retained as the strongest intended continuation; the alternatives lack supporting structure.

I rechecked the same-residue equations at n=4, 8, 12, and 16, including the 4r increment and modular differences, and tested all three modulus possibilities against the constrained coefficient values. The preceding proposal was not independently supported; the surviving continuation is three consecutive residues of -1, so Candidate 0 should be retained.
